item.title: Figma adds an AI assistant to its collaborative canvas
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Figma's new AI assistant will be first available on Figma Design

item.title: Quartermaster is building a maritime hive mind
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The Arlington, Virginia-based startup has raised $42 million to equip ships with sensors that blow the current AIS tech out of the water.

item.title: ‘Ask YouTube’ brings AI-powered conversational search to video, adds Gemini Omni to Shorts
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Google is completely revamping its search experience, and that doesn't stop at YouTube.

item.title: Google just declared itself a contender in AI design at IO 2026
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Google says it's designed the app to be accessible to everyone, from teachers to small business owners.

item.title: You can now talk to your Gmail inbox, as seen at Google IO 2026
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google expands Gmail’s AI Inbox with conversational voice search, letting users ask Gemini to find buried email details.

item.title: How to use Google’s new AI agents to go beyond your standard searches
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Google is launching AI-powered “information agents” that can monitor topics in the background and proactively alert users to updates and changes.

item.title: Discord enables end-to-end encrypted voice and video calling for every user
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Good news! Discord's hundreds of millions of users now have their communications scrambled, so not even Discord can see them.

item.title: Mach Industries just spent $50M to solve a major defense tech problem
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Mach says the acquisition meaningfully improves unit economics across its five vehicle programs at exactly the moment the company is starting to scale.

item.title: From teen hacker to Iron Dome researcher, this founder raised $28M to fight AI phishing
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Ocean, an agentic email security platform, claims its AI can thoroughly analyze the context of every incoming email to detect fraud and impersonation attempts.

item.title: Elon Musk said Sam Altman ‘stole’ a non-profit — but the trial showed he had similar aims
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The jury's speedy decision to reject Elon Musk's lawsuit against the other founders of OpenAI and Microsoft confirmed what we saw in the courtroom: Musk's case was a weak one, in part because he waited so long to file it.

item.title: Google takes a page out of Meta’s book, announces new audio-powered smart glasses at IO 2026
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Google is calling the new devices "audio glasses," in that users will be able to issue verbal commands to them and get things done via its ecosystem of apps and services, including Gemini.

item.title: Google’s Genie world model can now simulate real streets with Street View
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Google DeepMind is integrating Street View with Project Genie to create immersive, interactive world simulations for robotics, gaming, and travel, allowing users to explore environments, weather changes, and rare scenarios.

item.title: With Gemini 3.5 Flash, Google bets its next AI wave on agents, not chatbots
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Google launched Gemini 3.5 Flash, its most powerful coding and agentic AI model yet, at the company's annual developer conference. It is capable of autonomously executing complex tasks and building software from scratch.

item.title: Google Search as you know it is over
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google is transforming Search from a list of links into an AI-powered experience filled with conversational answers, autonomous agents, and interactive interfaces — a shift that could further reduce traffic to publishers across the web.

item.title: Google’s AI Studio now lets anyone build Android apps in minutes
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google unveiled new web-based AI tools that can generate native Android apps in minutes, as the company expands its push into AI-powered software development.

item.title: Google updates its Gemini app to take on ChatGPT and Claude at IO 2026
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The updates signal Google’s push to turn its Gemini app into an all-purpose AI hub rather than a stand-alone chatbot.

item.title: Agentic app coding gets an upgrade with Google’s release of Android CLI
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google is embracing the rise of AI coding agents with new Android tools designed to work with platforms like Claude Code and OpenAI’s Codex, allowing developers — or their AI assistants — to build Android apps faster from the command line.

item.title: Google’s new Universal Cart wants to follow your entire shopping journey across the internet
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Most people shop across multiple devices, many retailers, and over the course of many days, which is why Google is launching Universal Cart.

item.title: Google introduces Gemini Spark, a 24/7 agentic assistant with Gmail integration, at IO 2026
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: At the Google I/O developer conference, the company announced a new agentic personal assistant called Gemini Spark, built from Gemini's base models and an agentic harness from Google Antigravity.

item.title: Google adds voice-based prompting to Docs and Keep
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Google is letting users create drafts, take notes, and search for email with voice with the new Workspace update.
